description:
GOAL: First actual inlining. Caller's `Term::TailCall(F, args)` where F passes
is_inlinable is rewritten to a Goto into F's renamed entry, with F's
Return/Halt forwarded into the caller.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.1.

## Deliverables (extend src/ir_inline.rs)

  /// One pass over the module. For each fn C, for each block B with a
  /// TailCall(F, args) terminator where F.id != C.id and is_inlinable(F),
  /// rewrite via splice (recipe below). Returns total rewrites performed.
  /// Idempotent in the sense that re-running on an already-saturated module
  /// returns 0.
  pub fn inline_tail_calls_once(m: &mut Module) -> usize;

## Rewrite recipe

  Given: caller fn C, block B with terminator `TailCall(F, args)`. Both C, F
  in module m.

  1. Preconditions: F.id != C.id (self-recursion guard); is_inlinable(F).
     Reject if either fails.
  2. (F', vmap, bmap) = alpha_rename(F, &C).
  3. Walk F'.blocks; for each block, rewrite its terminator:
       Return(v')  ->  unchanged (caller's cont is what we'd have invoked
                       anyway; just propagate the value upward).
       Halt(v')    ->  unchanged.
       Goto/If     ->  unchanged (alpha_rename already updated their targets).
       Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure CANNOT appear — F is leaf.
  4. F'_entry = splice_blocks(&mut C, F').
  5. B's terminator becomes `Goto(F'_entry, args)`. (Note: F's entry-block
     params are now F'_entry's params; the Goto's args list aligns positionally
     with those params, satisfying the existing Goto-arity contract.)

## Self-recursion guard

  if F.id == C.id { return; }  // do not inline (even though leaf-fn property
  would have been false for a recursive fn anyway, this is defense-in-depth
  and makes intent explicit).

## Tests

  - Hand-built: `double(x) = x*2` (leaf, 2 stmts). Caller with
    `TailCall(double, [x])` → after pass, caller's terminator is
    `Goto(spliced_entry, [x])`; spliced body computes `x*2`; final terminator
    is `Return(...)`.
  - Self-recursion: a leaf fn that tail-calls itself (rare but possible) —
    NOT rewritten.
  - Non-leaf callee: a fn with internal Call — NOT rewritten.
  - Over-budget: callee with 9 Stmt::Lets — NOT rewritten.
  - ir_interp parity: a hand-built module with TailCall(F, x). Run interp
    on `main` pre- and post-inline; assert same Value.
  - Multiple inlinable sites in one caller: all rewritten in one pass.
  - Two callers of the same callee: both inlined; callee FnIr remains in
    module.fns (DCE is deferred to fz-ul4.11.28).

## Out of scope (this ticket)

  - Pipeline integration (compile() does NOT yet call this fn). End-to-end
    `fz run` behavior unchanged. Pipeline wiring is .15.5.

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes (no regressions).
  - New tests pass.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.2.

## References

  - fz-ul4.11.15 architecture section, Case 1.
  - ir_codegen::emit_tail_call (src/ir_codegen.rs:2185) — confirms cont_ptr
    is preserved in a TailCall, matching our "Return stays Return" recipe.
title: Inline TailCall sites (Case 1)
